result.content:
FINAL_ANSWER: The snake-like packing algorithm is flawed because it cannot guarantee adjacency preservation for high-degree nodes.
JUSTIFICATION: In a 2D grid, the maximum degree of any cell is 4. If the input topological map contains nodes (city regions) with a degree greater than 4 (star topologies), it is mathematically impossible to preserve all color-adjacency relations on a standard grid without violating the adjacency constraint or increasing the footprint.
